w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Dundee City Council, Executive Director of

Plane off the existing carriageway surface to a depth of 40mm across the full extents. A Dundee City Council Engineer will be present during operations to identify any localised areas requiring deeper excavation up to 100mm.
Regulate as required using Hot Rolled Asphalt (HRA) 30/14F surface regulating course (40/60 pen), applied in accordance with the stated conversion factor (0.43 m³/t).
Ensure pre-coated chippings are evenly distributed and fully embedded in accordance with specification.
Record and verify material temperatures on delivery and immediately prior to laying to confirm compliance with HRA laying temperature requirements.
Form smooth transition ramps at tie-in points to accommodate live traffic where required.
Maintain traffic flow between 15:30 and 07:30, ensuring safe and practical temporary running surfaces at all times.
Plane off the existing carriageway surface to an initial depth of
where required to achieve correct levels and profile.
Ensure all materials are laid within specified
, with checks undertaken at delivery and point of lay.
, with no segregation or surface defects.
During the day a temporary road surface should be available for the public to use as the road will return to normal use between
5 no. existing gully gratings to be adjusted and tied into new surface levels
All road markings to be installed as per the specifications provided in the lining diagrams referenced in the Master Lining Plan (
As part of the Pre-Construction Information (PCI) process, Dundee City Council has been notified of the proposed works. All residents and businesses along
have been contacted in advance of the commencement of construction activities to ensure proper communication and mitigate any potential disruptions.
will be completely shut with no access, however if there is the opportunity to leave
an access open for them then this would be preferable
Arrangements for security of the site:
Heras fencing to be erected around the perimeter of the site to
Ensure all excavations are covered or made safe at all times.
to members of the public for the duration of the works
Client to highlight site hazards and provide details under headings. Note that this is
Where appropriate, Client to provide existing Health & Safety File
No unusual slip or trip hazards. Contractor to ensure site is tidy at all times
, however the accuracy of the plans is unreliable and therefore the designs and construction should eliminate the potential impact with utilities by not exceeding
excavation depth to be minimised where practicable; trial holes and CAT scanning to be undertaken due to uncertain utility records
All excavations must be made safe when left unattended.
The Construction Phase Plan to include risk assessments, method statements and procedures to protect all personnel working or attending site.
Risks to members of the public, building users and others
Here’s a concise version of the information you provided, formatted for inclusion in a notice or communication:
These works will take place under a road closure and will be protected in accordance with the
Safety at Street Works and Road Works Code of Practice (CoP)
. Key details of the traffic management plan include:

oad to approx. the eastbound bus stop prior to
have all been informed that no access will be permitted between

is resurfaced, it should be opened for

will be maintained throughout the duration of the works.

Contact has been made by the client with each business, each business has been informed of no possible access between
pm throughout the duration of the works
be required to inform the residents of the scope of the works and times that they have to vacate the street. Info boards will be set out 7 days prior to start.
1. All waste material will be removed to the appropriate disposal site.
2. The works will be carried out under a full road closure to ensure the safety of the work force and members
3. To minimise inconvenience the surfacing works will be carried out under 2 phases.
4. The site has evidence of heave caused by tree routes, advice to be sought prior to works commencing.
drop from the client will be required to inform the residents of the scope of the works and times that
they have to vacate the street. Info boards will be set out 7 days prior to start.
Updates will be agreed with the client and approved in writing prior to implementation. The onsite information can be amended or updated via a mobile network and device.
The CCP will be updated in writing and saved on the M, drive the onsite copy will receive amendments via a mobile
All verbal instructions will also be confirmed in writing and the onsite folder updated to reflect any changes.
Communication will be both verbal and written. Changes to design or specification will be confirmed in writing once
necessary Risk assessments have been conducted and a safe method of work agreed.
The site chargehand will be in possession of a mobile device to review site files and drawings also the facility to
attend MS Teams meetings if the project engineer or client are unable to attend in person
Details of key members of Project Team
and have detailed above our control measures for the Construction Phase Plan.
